executive_summary.recommended_approach: AI-native Python pipeline controlled via Claude Code custom skills. Amazon Nova Canvas (primary) and Titan V2 (fallback) via AWS Bedrock in us-east-1, Pillow for text overlay, S3 for storage, CloudFormation for IaC, GitHub Actions for CI/CD, AWS MCP servers for natural language AWS administration. Designed for AI-assisted development speed and interview-quality demo readiness.
key_benefits: Claude Code custom skills replace all CLI/argparse interaction â€” zero UI development time, better developer experience
Nova Canvas (TIFA 0.897, ImageReward 1.250 per Amazon Nova Technical Report arxiv 2506.12103) available in us-east-1 â€” no cross-region dependency
3-tier model strategy: Nova Canvas ($0.04/img) for quality, Titan V2 ($0.01/img) for dev, with exponential retry/backoff
GitHub Actions CI/CD + Claude Code hooks enforce quality automatically â€” demonstrates engineering discipline
AWS MCP servers enable natural language AWS administration without leaving Claude Code
Flat JSON + S3 manifest eliminates database complexity â€” correct scope for PoC
key_risks: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (Anthropic) has an AWS Marketplace product ID — IAM user requires aws-marketplace:Subscribe for first-invocation auto-enablement. Amazon models (Nova Canvas, Titan V2) have no product ID and auto-enable on first invocation with no console action needed. G-001 gate verifies first invocation succeeds.
AnthropicBedrock client does not auto-read ~/.aws/config for region â€” must set AWS_REGION env var or aws_region constructor param
Text overlay via Pillow produces functional but not design-tool-quality typography â€” acceptable for PoC
Regulatory text placement on generated images requires coordinate tuning per aspect ratio
executive_summary.total_investment_range: Unconstrained â€” expected $5-25 GenAI API costs for PoC run. Nova Canvas $0.04/image (quality), Titan V2 $0.01/image (dev). AI-assisted development (Claude Code + Sonnet) handles all implementation.

llm.removed_model: stability.sd3-5-large-v1:0 â€” us-west-2 only, no cross-region inference profile, moved to BACKLOG
llm.rationale: Nova Canvas is the correct primary model: TIFA 0.897, ImageReward 1.250 per Amazon Nova Technical Report (arxiv 2506.12103v1) â€” highest available metrics for first-party AWS image models. Only available in us-east-1, which confirms region selection. SD3.5 Large is us-west-2 only with no cross-region inference profile â€” removing from design prevents region split. Titan V2 serves as dev/iterate fallback. Claude Sonnet 4.6 via AnthropicBedrock() provides text reasoning for prompt construction and compliance checks â€” same IAM credential chain as boto3, cleaner API.

orchestration.rationale: Linear pipeline (parse â†’ check assets â†’ construct prompt â†’ generate â†’ overlay â†’ upload â†’ manifest) is the simplest pattern. No LLM orchestration, RAG, or agent loops. Direct boto3 and anthropic[bedrock] calls. Adding LangChain or similar would be over-engineering for a PoC.

backend.framework: Claude Code custom skills (no argparse or Click)

frontend.framework: Claude Code CLI with custom skills
frontend.rationale: 8 custom skills in .claude/skills/ replace all CLI interaction. Skills are invoked from Claude Code, which provides context, argument parsing, and human-readable output. Zero UI development time. Better interview demo experience â€” developer can narrate while running /run-pipeline.

input_assets.purpose: Brand asset storage â€” logos, product photos, templates. Block Public Access enabled.

output_packaging.purpose: Generated packaging images organized by SKU/Region/format. Block Public Access enabled.

pipeline_manifest.purpose: Run history for /pipeline-status skill. No database required for PoC.
pipeline_manifest.block_public_access: N/A (S3 bucket inherits Block Public Access)

infrastructure.compute: Local Python execution via Claude Code skills (Lambda-ready for future deployment)
infrastructure.region_rationale: us-east-1 selected because Amazon Nova Canvas (primary image model) is only available in us-east-1. Titan V2 and Claude Sonnet 4.6 also available in us-east-1. SD3.5 Large (us-west-2 only) removed from design â€” no cross-region inference profile exists.
infrastructure.monitoring: CloudWatch basic metrics (PoC scope) + structured JSON logs
infrastructure.logging: Structured JSON to stdout + pipeline run manifest to S3

ci_cd.ci_workflow: .github/workflows/ci.yml â€” triggers on every push; runs ruff lint, pytest unit tests, pip-audit
ci_cd.deploy_workflow: .github/workflows/deploy.yml â€” triggers on main push; runs CloudFormation deploy
ci_cd.security: All action versions pinned to SHA hashes. AWS credentials via GitHub Secrets or OIDC federation.
ci_cd.rationale: CI/CD is must-have (FR-009). Demonstrates engineering discipline. GitHub Actions is zero-cost for public repos.

mcp_servers.transport: stdio (uv tool run â€” Python packages, not npm)

active_servers.name: AWS IaC MCP Server
active_servers.purpose: CloudFormation and CDK assistance, template validation, cfn-lint, cfn-guard

active_servers.name: AWS Knowledge MCP Server
active_servers.purpose: AWS documentation, regional availability, CloudFormation resource knowledge
active_servers.stdio_command: uvx fastmcp run https://knowledge-mcp.global.api.aws (fastmcp proxy for stdio clients)

component_design.name: Claude Code Interface Layer
component_design.technology: Claude Code CLI (.claude/skills/ in target repo)
component_design.purpose: 8 custom skills replace all CLI/argparse interaction. Skills provide: pipeline invocation, status display, result viewing, infrastructure operations, health checks, and demo generation. No argparse or Click framework.

component_design.name: SKU Brief Parser
component_design.purpose: Validate SKU brief JSON against src/schemas/sku_brief_schema.json, extract products, regions, attributes, and audience data. Sanitize all string fields before use in prompt templates (prompt injection mitigation).
inputs: Raw SKU brief JSON file path
outputs: Validated SKUBrief dict with products, region, audience, attributes
component_design.security_note: All SKU brief string fields sanitized before prompt interpolation â€” maximum field lengths enforced per schema.

component_design.name: Asset Manager
component_design.purpose: Check S3 input bucket for existing brand assets (logos, product photos). Download and cache locally if found. Report missing assets to trigger generation. Build S3 output key hierarchy.
inputs: S3 bucket configuration (from CloudFormation stack outputs)

component_design.name: Prompt Constructor
component_design.technology: Python (f-string templates) + C-012 (optional text reasoning for creative variants)
component_design.purpose: Build GenAI image generation prompts from SKU brief data â€” product name, attributes, region/cultural context, aspect ratio, and packaging type. For basic generation: f-string templates (zero cost). For brand voice adaptation or compliance text: delegate to C-012 (AnthropicBedrock).
outputs: Prompt string for Nova Canvas / Titan V2
Text overlay content dict (title, attributes, regulatory_text)

component_design.name: Image Generator
component_design.purpose: Call Amazon Bedrock to generate packaging images. 2-tier model strategy with exponential retry/backoff and local cache. Dry-run mode returns 1x1 pixel placeholder (zero API cost).

model_tiers.dev: amazon.titan-image-generator-v2:0 ($0.01/image) â€” pipeline development and debugging

retry_backoff.jitter: random(0, base_delay * 0.3) added to each base delay â€” prevents thundering herd under concurrent load
retry_backoff.fallback_on_exhaustion: Fall from Nova Canvas to Titan V2 after 3 failures. Log model tier used per image.

caching.key: SHA-256 hash of (prompt + width + height + model_id)
caching.effect: Skip Bedrock call on cache hit â€” reduces cost during debug iterations

dry_run.trigger: dry_run=True parameter or PAI_DRY_RUN=1 env var
dry_run.behavior: Return 1x1 pixel placeholder PNG bytes â€” zero Bedrock API cost
dry_run.use_case: Validate SKU brief schema, S3 connectivity, and prompt construction without incurring costs

component_design.scalability: Parallelizable via concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor â€” generate multiple images simultaneously

component_design.name: Text Overlay Engine
component_design.purpose: Composite product messaging, regulatory information, and attribute badges onto generated packaging images. Handle font sizing, positioning, and readability per aspect ratio. Use bundled TTF font (not Windows-specific system fonts) for cross-platform portability.
inputs: Text overlay content dict (title, attributes, regulatory_text)
outputs: Final composited packaging image bytes with text overlays

layout_rules.1:1: Product name top-third centered, attributes center, regulatory text bottom strip
layout_rules.9:16: Product name top, description mid, regulatory text bottom
layout_rules.16:9: Product name left-aligned top, attributes right panel, regulatory text bottom strip

component_design.scalability: CPU-bound but fast â€” sub-second per image

component_design.name: Output Manager
component_design.purpose: Upload final packaging images to S3 with organized key hierarchy. Write pipeline run manifest to both S3 and local filesystem. Manifest enables /pipeline-status skill without a database.
outputs: S3 object keys for all uploaded files
Pipeline run manifest (E-006) written to S3 + local

component_design.name: IaC Stack
component_design.purpose: Define S3 buckets (input + output with Block Public Access), IAM role with least-privilege Bedrock and S3 access. Self-contained single YAML file. Outputs: bucket names and role ARN consumed by pipeline.

resources: S3 input bucket with BlockPublicAcls: true, BlockPublicPolicy: true, IgnorePublicAcls: true, RestrictPublicBuckets: true
S3 output bucket with same Block Public Access settings

IAM user policy: bedrock:InvokeModel + bedrock:InvokeModelWithResponseStream on 3 foundation-model ARNs; s3:ListBucket on bucket ARNs; s3:GetObject/PutObject on object ARNs; CloudFormation all actions Resource:* (changeset ARN format differs from stack ARN); IAM role/profile management scoped to pai-exercise-* ARNs; budgets:ModifyBudget/ViewBudget; aws-marketplace:Subscribe/Unsubscribe/ViewSubscriptions for Anthropic auto-enablement
IAM instance profile (for future Lambda execution)
cloudformation_spec.deploy_command: aws cloudformation deploy --stack-name pai-exercise --template-file infra/cloudformation/stack.yaml --capabilities CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM --profile pai-exercise

component_design.security_note: S3 Block Public Access enabled on all 4 settings for both buckets (closes F-001). IAM policy uses least privilege — no wildcard resources except CloudFormation (required: changeset ARNs differ from stack ARNs) and Budgets/Marketplace (no resource-level support). CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM required for named role. aws-marketplace:Subscribe scoped to Anthropic first-time auto-enablement.

component_design.name: Claude Code Hooks
component_design.purpose: Enforce quality automatically: auto-lint changed files on every edit, run tests before completing any task, guard against destructive AWS commands without confirmation.

hooks.action: Auto-lint changed Python files with ruff check {changed_file}. Block on lint errors.
Run pytest tests/ -v -m 'not integration'. Block task completion if tests fail.
Pattern match for destructive AWS commands (cloudformation delete-stack, s3 rm, iam delete-*). Require explicit confirmation before proceeding.

component_design.name: GitHub Actions CI/CD
component_design.purpose: Enforce lint, test, and security checks on every push. Deploy CloudFormation on main push. Demonstrates engineering discipline for interview panel.

workflows.trigger: push to any branch, pull_request to main

workflows.aws_credentials: GitHub Secret: AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID + AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY (or OIDC federation)

component_design.security: All action versions pinned to SHA hashes (supply chain protection). No credential echo in logs.
component_design.scalability: GitHub-managed â€” zero infrastructure cost for public repo

component_design.name: AWS MCP Servers
component_design.purpose: Enable natural language AWS administration from Claude Code: CloudFormation template validation, IaC assistance, AWS documentation lookup. Uses uv tool run (NOT npm/npx â€” AWS MCP servers are Python packages).
component_design.prerequisites: uv (Python package manager) installed. Node.js not required.

component_design.name: Text Reasoning Engine
component_design.purpose: Optional text reasoning for prompt template enhancement, brand voice adaptation, and compliance text generation. Uses same IAM credential chain as boto3. Called by C-004 when creative or compliance-aware text is needed.
component_design.client_code: from anthropic import AnthropicBedrock; client = AnthropicBedrock(aws_region='us-east-1')
component_design.credential_chain: Same as boto3 â€” reads AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, AWS_SESSION_TOKEN. Does NOT read ~/.aws/config for region â€” must set aws_region param or AWS_REGION env var.
use_cases: Construct culturally-adapted packaging copy from SKU brief attributes
Generate regulatory compliance text templates per region
Evaluate generated image descriptions for brand voice consistency (Phase 6)
outputs: Generated text (product copy, regulatory text, compliance assessment)

data_flows.name: Asset Lookup
data_flows.encryption: TLS in transit, SSE-S3 at rest

data_flows.name: Image Generation Request
data_flows.destination: Amazon Bedrock us-east-1 (Nova Canvas / Titan V2)
data_flows.data_type: JSON request â†’ Base64 PNG response

data_flows.name: Text Reasoning Request
data_flows.destination: Amazon Bedrock us-east-1 (Claude Sonnet 4.6 via AnthropicBedrock)
data_flows.note: Optional â€” only called when creative text adaptation is needed

data_flows.name: Output Upload
data_flows.encryption: TLS in transit, SSE-S3 at rest

system_context.code:
flowchart LR
    DEV["Developer"]
    CC["Claude Code CLI"]
    SKU["SKU Brief JSON"]
    PIPELINE["Packaging Pipeline"]
    S3_IN["S3 Input Bucket"]
    BEDROCK["Amazon Bedrock"]
    S3_OUT["S3 Output Bucket"]
    GHA["GitHub Actions"]
    MCP["AWS MCP Servers"]

    DEV -->|"invokes skill"| CC
    CC -->|"runs"| PIPELINE
    SKU -->|"input"| PIPELINE
    PIPELINE -->|"checks assets"| S3_IN
    S3_IN -->|"brand assets"| PIPELINE
    PIPELINE -->|"generates images"| BEDROCK
    BEDROCK -->|"base64 PNG"| PIPELINE
    PIPELINE -->|"saves outputs"| S3_OUT
    DEV -->|"git push"| GHA
    GHA -->|"deploys CFN"| S3_OUT
    DEV -->|"AWS admin"| MCP

data_flow.code:
flowchart TB
    INPUT["SKU Brief JSON"]
    PARSE["C-002: Parse and Validate"]
    ASSET_CHECK["C-003: Check S3 Assets"]
    PROMPT["C-004: Construct Prompts"]
    GENERATE["C-005: Generate via Bedrock"]
    OVERLAY["C-006: Text Overlay via Pillow"]
    OUTPUT["C-007: Output Manager"]
    S3["S3 Output Bucket"]
    MANIFEST["manifest.json"]

    INPUT --> PARSE
    PARSE --> ASSET_CHECK
    ASSET_CHECK -->|"missing assets"| PROMPT
    ASSET_CHECK -->|"found assets"| OVERLAY
    PROMPT --> GENERATE
    GENERATE -->|"retry on throttle"| GENERATE
    GENERATE --> OVERLAY
    OVERLAY --> OUTPUT
    OUTPUT --> S3
    OUTPUT --> MANIFEST

deployment.code:
flowchart TB
    subgraph "Developer Machine"
        CC["Claude Code + Skills"]
        PYTHON["Python 3.12 Pipeline"]
        PILLOW["Pillow Library"]
        MCP["AWS MCP Servers"]
    end
    subgraph "AWS us-east-1"
        IAM["IAM Role"]
        S3_ASSETS["S3: Asset Bucket"]
        S3_OUTPUT["S3: Output Bucket"]

        BEDROCK_TV["Titan V2"]
        BEDROCK_CL["Claude Sonnet 4.6"]
    end
    subgraph "GitHub"
        GHA_CI["ci.yml: lint + test"]
        GHA_DEPLOY["deploy.yml: CFN"]
    end

    CC --> PYTHON
    PYTHON --> PILLOW
    PYTHON -->|"boto3 HTTPS"| S3_ASSETS
    PYTHON -->|"boto3 HTTPS"| S3_OUTPUT
    PYTHON -->|"boto3 InvokeModel"| BEDROCK_NC
    PYTHON -->|"boto3 InvokeModel"| BEDROCK_TV
    PYTHON -->|"anthropic[bedrock]"| BEDROCK_CL
    MCP -->|"uv stdio"| S3_ASSETS
    IAM -->|"least privilege"| S3_ASSETS
    IAM -->|"least privilege"| S3_OUTPUT
    IAM -->|"least privilege"| BEDROCK_NC
    GHA_CI -->|"on push"| GHA_DEPLOY
    GHA_DEPLOY -->|"on main"| S3_OUTPUT

services.compute: Local Python execution via Claude Code skills (Lambda-ready for future deployment)
services.storage: Amazon S3 â€” 2 buckets (input assets, output packaging) both with Block Public Access
services.ai_ml: Amazon Bedrock us-east-1 â€” Nova Canvas (primary, $0.04/img), Titan V2 (fallback, $0.01/img), Claude Sonnet 4.6 (text reasoning)
services.mcp: AWS IaC MCP Server + AWS Knowledge MCP Server

cloud_infrastructure.deployment_pattern: Skills-first with CI/CD enforcement. Pipeline invoked via Claude Code /run-pipeline skill during development and demo. GitHub Actions enforces lint, tests, and CloudFormation deploy on main.

monitoring.logging: Structured JSON to stdout + pipeline run manifest (E-006) to S3 + local
monitoring.alerting: AWS Budget alarm at $10 spend â€” production would add CloudWatch Alarms on Bedrock error rates

backup_dr.strategy: S3 versioning enabled on output bucket. Manifest.json written per run enables rerun from SKU brief. No cross-region replication for PoC.

cost_optimization.strategy: 2-tier model cost strategy: Titan V2 ($0.01/image) for dev tier, Nova Canvas ($0.04/image) for iterate and final tiers. Dry-run mode for zero-cost validation. Cache generated images locally. Budget unconstrained â€” quality is optimization target.
cost_optimization.estimated_run_cost: $5-25 for full PoC demo run (4 SKU briefs Ã— 2 products Ã— 3 ratios = 24 images at final tier = ~$1 Nova Canvas; iterative dev runs at Titan V2 = $0.01/image)
cost_levers: Default to Titan V2 ($0.01) during pipeline development â€” 4x cheaper than Nova Canvas
Use --dry-run for pipeline validation â€” zero Bedrock cost
Cache hit avoids re-generation during text overlay iteration
AWS Budget alarm at $10 to catch runaway API calls

never_do: Use default profile in production scripts
credential_management.github_actions: Use GitHub Secrets for AWS credentials (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID + AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY) or OIDC federation for keyless authentication
credential_management.mcp_servers: Pass AWS_PROFILE and AWS_REGION in env block of .mcp.json server config
credential_management.anthropic_bedrock: AnthropicBedrock() reads AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID and AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY from environment. Set aws_region='us-east-1' explicitly (does not read ~/.aws/config).
credential_management.security_note: Closes F-002 from v1.0 security review. Named profiles prevent accidental credential exposure.

ai_ml_components.pattern: Simple GenAI Wrapper â€” direct API call pipeline with retry/backoff. No orchestration framework or agent loops.

image_generation.removed: stability.sd3-5-large-v1:0 â€” us-west-2 only, no cross-region inference profile
image_generation.model_selection_rationale: Nova Canvas selected: TIFA 0.897, ImageReward 1.250 per Amazon Nova Technical Report (arxiv 2506.12103v1). Only available in us-east-1, which confirms region choice. SD3.5 Large removed â€” only in us-west-2 with no cross-region inference profile (confirmed via AWS docs March 2026). Text rendering limitations of both models are mitigated by Pillow text overlay â€” all label copy applied programmatically, not via GenAI text generation.
image_generation.retry_backoff: 3 attempts, exponential (2s, 4s, 8s), ThrottlingException + ServiceUnavailableException retryable. Fall to Titan V2 after 3 Nova Canvas failures.

text_reasoning.iam: bedrock:InvokeModel â€” same as image generation role
text_reasoning.note: AWS_REGION env var or aws_region constructor param required â€” AnthropicBedrock does not auto-read ~/.aws/config

operations: Text overlay: product name, key attributes, regulatory information
Font: bundled TTF (not Windows-specific â€” portability requirement)
Layout zones: designated areas per aspect ratio for text placement
Font sizing: auto-scale based on image dimensions and text length

responsible_ai.human_oversight: Human review required before any client-facing packaging output. /pipeline-status skill enables audit trail. Manifest records model tier and generation metadata.
responsible_ai.bias_mitigation: Regional imagery prompts reviewed during demo construction. No automated bias detection at PoC scale.
responsible_ai.transparency: Model tier used (Nova Canvas vs Titan V2) logged in every manifest entry.
responsible_ai.hallucination_risk: Low â€” regulatory text and product copy applied via Pillow (code), not generated by model. Model generates background imagery only.
responsible_ai.model_selection_criteria: NFR-008 in requirements.json â€” selection justified by benchmark evidence (TIFA, ImageReward from Amazon Nova Technical Report arxiv 2506.12103v1)
responsible_ai.model_access_control: IAM least-privilege: bedrock:InvokeModel on specific foundation-model ARNs only
responsible_ai.prompt_injection_protection: All SKU brief string fields sanitized (max length enforced, special characters stripped) before interpolation into prompt templates

logging.format: Structured JSON logs (Python logging with json formatter)
logging.levels: INFO for pipeline steps, WARNING for model tier fallback, ERROR for failures
logging.correlation_id: UUID per pipeline run, propagated through all log entries
logging.retention: Local stdout for skill invocation; manifest.json persisted to S3 per run

ai_specific: Model tier used (Nova Canvas / Titan V2)
metrics.collection: Written to pipeline run manifest (E-006) at completion â€” no real-time dashboard for PoC

tracing.strategy: run_id (UUID4) per pipeline run, written to manifest.json and all log entries. Skill invocation log (E-007) links run_id to Claude Code session.

well_architected_scores.score_source: parallel-wa-reviewer sub-agents (6 parallel assessments via /review R-004)
well_architected_scores.calibration_note: Scores reflect independent parallel agent review, not self-assessment. Self-assessment scored 7.7; parallel review scored 6.8. Discrepancy is driven by PoC gaps in observability, jitter, parallelism, and per-call timeouts â€” all appropriate deferrals for a PoC scope. Production path to 8.5+ documented in BACKLOG.

strengths: IaC from day one via CloudFormation
GitHub Actions CI/CD enforces quality on every push
Claude Code hooks auto-lint and auto-test (PostToolUse, Stop)
Structured JSON logs with correlation IDs per run
Pipeline run manifest enables operational visibility without a database
Claude Code skills provide consistent, documented interface
gaps: No CloudWatch log group or dashboards â€” metrics written to manifest only
No operational runbooks or incident response procedures
Local image cache has no TTL or invalidation strategy
No ORR (Operational Readiness Review) gate before release
recommendations: Add CloudWatch log group to CloudFormation stack for pipeline invocations
Document cache invalidation: TTL-based expiry or explicit --clear-cache flag
Add basic runbook in BACKLOG for production path

strengths: IAM least-privilege roles scoped to S3 and Bedrock only
SSE-S3 encryption at rest on all buckets
S3 Block Public Access enabled on all 4 settings for both buckets (closes F-001)
AWS CLI named profiles â€” no hardcoded credentials (closes F-002)
pip-audit in CI for dependency vulnerability scanning (closes F-003)
All GitHub Actions versions pinned to SHA hashes (supply chain protection)
Prompt injection mitigation: SKU brief string fields sanitized before interpolation
gaps: Bedrock invocation logging not enabled (no CloudTrail data events for model calls â€” F-004 open)
OIDC federation recommended but Secrets-based credentials are the default CI path
MCP server packages run with uv (internet-fetched) â€” no version pinning in .mcp.json
recommendations: Enable Bedrock model invocation logging to S3 in production
Pin awslabs MCP package version in .mcp.json (e.g., awslabs.aws-iac-mcp-server==0.1.2)
Set OIDC as default CI credential method â€” document Secrets as fallback only

strengths: Exponential retry/backoff with jitter: 3 attempts, ThrottlingException + ServiceUnavailableException retryable (closes RF-009)
2-tier model fallback: Nova Canvas â†’ Titan V2 after 3 failures
Dry-run mode validates pipeline without Bedrock calls (closes RF-010)
Local cache (~/.cache/pai-pipeline/) enables resume after failures
Manifest written per run enables replay from SKU brief
Stateless pipeline â€” safe to retry from any stage
gaps: No per-call Bedrock invocation timeout (boto3 default is no timeout â€” risk of indefinite hang)
RTO/RPO not defined â€” explicitly undefined for PoC but should be stated
No stage checkpointing between pipeline stages for partial run recovery
recommendations: Add boto3 Config(connect_timeout=5, read_timeout=120) to Bedrock client
State explicitly: RTO=best-effort, RPO=none (no stateful data at risk) for PoC
Add stage checkpointing in Phase 3 (write each image to local before S3 upload)

strengths: Right-sized compute (local execution, no over-provisioning)
Managed AI inference via Bedrock eliminates GPU management
Local image cache eliminates redundant Bedrock calls
2-tier model strategy matches cost to quality need
gaps: Sequential image generation â€” 6 images at 30s each = ~3 minutes, likely violates p95 NFR â‰¤60s for multi-product runs
No performance instrumentation beyond total duration_seconds in manifest
No per-image timing logged (impossible to identify slow requests)
recommendations: Add concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor for parallel image generation (Phase 3 or BACKLOG)
Add per-image duration_ms to manifest E-006 for granular performance tracking
Note p95 NFR as best-effort for PoC; requires parallel execution for production compliance

cost_optimization.delta: Revised from self-assessed 8 to parallel-agent 9 â€” tiered model strategy is genuinely strong
strengths: Zero idle compute (on-demand execution via Claude Code skills)
2-tier model pricing ($0.01 Titan V2 dev / $0.04 Nova Canvas final)
Dry-run mode enables zero-cost pipeline validation
Local cache avoids re-generation during text overlay iteration â€” amortizes per-run cost
IaC enables clean teardown to zero ongoing cost
AWS Budget alarm planned at $10 provides runaway cost protection
gaps: No S3 lifecycle policy confirmed in CloudFormation template
recommendations: Add S3 lifecycle expiration (90 days) in CloudFormation template
Consider Nova Canvas Standard vs. Premium tiers if available for further cost optimization

sustainability.delta: Confirmed by parallel-agent review (no change from self-assessed 7)
strengths: On-demand execution eliminates idle waste
Bedrock shared infrastructure amortizes GPU utilization across AWS fleet
Local cache avoids redundant GenAI calls â€” directly reduces energy consumption
IaC teardown eliminates zombie resources
gaps: us-east-1 (N. Virginia) has lower renewable energy % than us-west-2 (Oregon)
recommendations: Add S3 lifecycle expiration policy (90 days) in CloudFormation
AWS 100% renewable energy commitment globally mitigates regional gap at fleet level
Document cache hit rate in manifest to quantify energy savings from reuse

alternatives_considered.name: Amazon Nova Canvas â€” SELECTED AS PRIMARY
alternatives_considered.summary: First-party AWS image model. TIFA 0.897, ImageReward 1.250. Only available in us-east-1. $0.04/image. Built-in inpainting/outpainting for future enhancement.
alternatives_considered.why_rejected: N/A â€” selected as primary model. Confirms us-east-1 as correct region.

alternatives_considered.name: Stable Diffusion 3.5 Large (stability.sd3-5-large-v1:0)
alternatives_considered.summary: 8B parameter model, $0.08/image. Only available in us-west-2. No cross-region inference profile.
alternatives_considered.why_rejected: Region conflict: us-west-2 only, no cross-region inference. Nova Canvas available in us-east-1 with better benchmark scores on available metrics. SD3.5 moved to BACKLOG for future consideration if cross-region inference becomes available.

alternatives_considered.name: Amazon Titan Image Generator V2 â€” SELECTED AS FALLBACK
alternatives_considered.summary: Available in us-east-1. $0.01/image (4x cheaper than Nova Canvas). Lower quality but adequate for dev/iterate tier.
alternatives_considered.why_rejected: N/A â€” retained as fallback and dev tier model.

alternatives_considered.name: argparse / Click CLI
alternatives_considered.summary: Traditional Python CLI framework. Required for v1.0 design.
alternatives_considered.why_rejected: Replaced by Claude Code custom skills. Skills provide better developer experience, zero additional framework dependency, and demonstrate AI-native development approach.

alternatives_considered.name: PostgreSQL on RDS
alternatives_considered.summary: Relational database for run history and manifest storage.
alternatives_considered.why_rejected: Over-engineering for PoC. Flat JSON manifest files + S3 provide adequate run history for /pipeline-status skill. RDS adds cost, complexity, and setup time. Moved to BACKLOG.

alternatives_considered.name: AWS CDK instead of CloudFormation
alternatives_considered.summary: Infrastructure as code using Python CDK constructs.
alternatives_considered.why_rejected: CDK adds npm dependency and synthesis step. CloudFormation YAML is self-contained, simpler for a small stack (2 buckets + 1 IAM role). AWS IaC MCP Server validates CloudFormation templates directly.

alternatives_considered.name: boto3 bedrock-runtime for Claude calls
alternatives_considered.summary: Direct boto3 InvokeModel for Claude Sonnet via Bedrock.
alternatives_considered.why_rejected: anthropic[bedrock] provides much cleaner API: client.messages.create() vs invoke_model() + JSON parsing. Same IAM permission (bedrock:InvokeModel). Same credential chain. Preferred for text reasoning component.

_metadata.author: sa-agent
_metadata.change_log: v2.0: Primary model changed from SD3.5 Large to Nova Canvas (SD3.5 us-west-2 only â€” no cross-region inference). Region changed from us-west-2 to us-east-1 (Nova Canvas availability). C-001 argparse replaced by Claude Code skills. C-009 hooks, C-010 CI/CD, C-011 MCP servers, C-012 text reasoning added. Retry/backoff added to C-005 (closes RF-009). S3 Block Public Access added to C-008 (closes F-001). Credential management documented (closes F-002). Dry-run mode documented (closes RF-010). WA scores updated: OpEx 7â†’8, Security 7â†’8, Reliability 6â†’8, Cost 9â†’8 (unconstrained budget), Sustainability 8â†’7 (us-west-2â†’us-east-1). Overall 7.3â†’7.7.
_metadata.notes: v2.1: Bedrock console enablement retired — Amazon models auto-enable on first invocation; Anthropic models require aws-marketplace:Subscribe. IAM policy and G-001 gate updated accordingly. v2.2: Corrected IAM policy: added InvokeModelWithResponseStream, removed invalid budgets:CreateBudget/DeleteBudget, fixed CloudFormation to Resource:* for changeset ARN compatibility, split S3 bucket vs object ARN scopes, updated deploy command to CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM.
